From: "National Fraud Awareness Center" To: "Lawrence Mendoza" Subject: Dating Scam Alert: Protect your heart and connection from romance fraud — Guidance for 2087 Date: Fri, 11 Jul 2087 14:48:46 +0200 MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Dating Scam Alert

National Fraud Awareness Center — Safety Notice

Dear Lawrence,

We are contacting you because recent reports show an increase in romance-related scams targeting residents in Virginia Beach and surrounding areas. Scammers know how to play on feelings of being lonely or wanting a special connection — they craft stories to win your heart before asking for money or financial help.

Why this matters:
  • Romance scams (sometimes called pig butchering) involve lengthy grooming to build trust and emotional connection.
  • Scammers often ask for transfers, investments, or help moving funds through third-party services.
  • If someone pressures you or tells an urgent personal story to obtain money, it is very likely a scam.

We know how meaningful a new connection can feel. Scammers count on those emotions — the hope of something special, the relief from feeling lonely — to manipulate victims. Please be cautious with anyone who:

  • Moves communication off a verified dating platform very quickly.
  • Requests money, gift cards, cryptocurrency, or asks you to click a link to "verify" or "unlock" funds.
  • Claims an emergency (medical, legal, travel) and requests immediate payment.

If you have received a message that makes you uncomfortable or asks you to send funds, do not respond and do not click any suspicious links. Instead, take these immediate steps:

  1. Stop communication with the person and preserve the messages as evidence.
  2. Do not share personal or financial information.
  3. Report the user to the dating platform and to authorities via official resources below.

Helpful resources:

  • Federal Trade Commission — Report Fraud
  • FBI — Internet Crime Complaint Center (IC3)
  • IdentityTheft.gov — Recovery Steps

If you believe you are already a victim, report it right away. Immediate reporting increases the chance of recovery and helps protect others from the same criminals.

We created a short checklist tailored for Virginia Beach residents that you can download and share with family members who may feel lonely or are new to online dating. Stay alert, protect your heart, and keep your connections safe.

Read the Romance Scam Checklist

Sincerely,
Amelia Rowe
Community Outreach Coordinator — National Fraud Awareness Center
